message:
fix(tutorial): address code review issues in context and layout

Bug fixes from fresh-eyes code review:

1. tutorial-context.tsx:
   - Remove type casting hack (as unknown as) in TutorialProvider
   - Use proper null coalescing (initialPath ?? null) instead
   - Preserve startedAt timestamp across saves using ref
   - Previously, every state change overwrote startedAt, losing
     when the user actually started the tutorial
   - Add startedAtRef management to setPath, resetProgress,
     and clearAllProgress functions

2. layout.tsx:
   - Remove duplicate lg:pl-72 class in main content styling
   - Remove redundant lg:hidden from MobileHeader (parent already
     has md:hidden which takes effect first)
   - Add clarifying comments for responsive layout logic

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"brenner_bot-1v26.1","title":"Define Session Data Model & TypeScript Interfaces","description":"# Define Session Data Model & TypeScript Interfaces

## Purpose
Create the foundational TypeScript interfaces that define the entire session data structure.

## Why First
Everything else builds on these types. Getting them right early prevents painful refactoring later.

## Key Interfaces to Define

### Session (top-level)
```typescript
interface Session {
  id: string;
  createdAt: Date;
  updatedAt: Date;
  phase: SessionPhase;
  
  hypothesis: HypothesisCard;
  hypothesisHistory: HypothesisVersion[];
  
  operatorApplications: {
    levelSplit?: LevelSplitResult;
    exclusionTest?: ExclusionTestResult;
    objectTranspose?: ObjectTransposeResult;
    scaleCheck?: ScaleCheckResult;
  };
  
  agentResponses: AgentResponse[];
  synthesis?: SynthesisResult;
  
  evidenceLedger: EvidenceEntry[];
  artifacts: ResearchArtifact[];
  
  // Git-style versioning
  commits: SessionCommit[];
}
```

### SessionPhase
```typescript
type SessionPhase =
  | 'intake'
  | 'sharpening'
  | 'level_split'
  | 'exclusion_test'
  | 'object_transpose'
  | 'scale_check'
  | 'agent_dispatch'
  | 'synthesis'
  | 'evidence_gathering'
  | 'revision'
  | 'complete';
```

### HypothesisCard, EvidenceEntry, etc.
Define all the nested types from the epic descriptions.

## File Location
`apps/web/src/lib/brenner-loop/types.ts`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All core interfaces defined with JSDoc comments
- [ ] Exported properly for use across the app
- [ ] Type guards for runtime validation
- [ ] Discriminated unions where appropriate (SessionPhase, etc.)

 {"id":"brenner_bot-1v26.2","title":"Implement LocalStorage Session Persistence","description":"# Implement LocalStorage Session Persistence

## Purpose
Create the first persistence layer using browser localStorage. This enables:
- Sessions survive browser refresh
- No backend required for MVP
- Works offline

## API Design
```typescript
interface SessionStorage {
  save(session: Session): Promise<void>;
  load(sessionId: string): Promise<Session | null>;
  list(): Promise<SessionSummary[]>;
  delete(sessionId: string): Promise<void>;
}

// Summary for list view (don't load full session)
interface SessionSummary {
  id: string;
  hypothesis: string;  // Short preview
  phase: SessionPhase;
  confidence: number;
  updatedAt: Date;
}
```

## Implementation Details
- Use localStorage with JSON serialization
- Key pattern: `brenner-session-{id}`
- Index key: `brenner-sessions-index`
- Handle storage quota limits gracefully
- Auto-save on state change (debounced)

## Migration Path
Design so we can later add:
- IndexedDB for larger storage
- Remote sync (Supabase)
- Git-backed storage (Agent Mail style)

## Edge Cases
- Storage full → warn user, offer cleanup
- Corrupted data → recover what's possible
- Concurrent tabs → handle conflicts

## File Location
`apps/web/src/lib/brenner-loop/storage.ts`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Save/load round-trips correctly
- [ ] List shows all sessions with summaries
- [ ] Handles storage limits gracefully
- [ ] Works offline

 {"id":"brenner_bot-1v26.3","title":"Create Session Context Provider & Hooks","description":"# Create Session Context Provider & Hooks

## Purpose
Provide React context and hooks for accessing and manipulating session state throughout the UI.

## Design
```typescript
// Context
interface SessionContextValue {
  session: Session | null;
  isLoading: boolean;
  error: Error | null;
  
  // Actions
  createSession(initialHypothesis: string): Promise<Session>;
  loadSession(id: string): Promise<void>;
  updateHypothesis(updates: Partial<HypothesisCard>): void;
  advancePhase(): void;
  goToPhase(phase: SessionPhase): void;
  
  // Computed
  canAdvance: boolean;
  availablePhases: SessionPhase[];
}

// Provider
function SessionProvider({ children }: { children: ReactNode }): JSX.Element;

// Hooks
function useSession(): SessionContextValue;
function useHypothesis(): HypothesisCard | null;
function useCurrentPhase(): SessionPhase;
function usePhaseNavigation(): { next: () => void; prev: () => void; canNext: boolean; canPrev: boolean };
```

## Implementation Notes
- Use useReducer for complex state management
- Auto-save to storage on state changes
- Debounce saves to avoid thrashing
- Handle hydration from localStorage on mount

## File Location
`apps/web/src/lib/brenner-loop/session-context.tsx`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Context provides all session state
- [ ] Hooks work correctly
- [ ] Auto-saves on changes
- [ ] Handles loading/error states

-{"id":"brenner_bot-1v26.4","title":"Implement Session Export/Import","description":"# Implement Session Export/Import

## Purpose
Allow users to export sessions for backup, sharing, or moving between devices.

## Export Formats

### JSON (Full Fidelity)
```typescript
interface SessionExport {
  format: 'brenner-session-v1';
  exportedAt: Date;
  session: Session;
  checksum: string;  // For integrity verification
}
```

### Markdown (Human Readable)
- Convert session to readable markdown
- Include hypothesis card, operator results, evidence
- Not re-importable (one-way)

## Implementation
```typescript
async function exportSession(
  session: Session,
  format: 'json' | 'markdown'
): Promise<Blob>;

async function importSession(
  file: File
): Promise<{ session: Session; warnings: string[] }>;
```

## Import Validation
- Check format version compatibility
- Verify checksum
- Validate all required fields
- Handle missing optional fields gracefully
- Return warnings for any issues (don't fail silently)

## UI Integration
- Export button in session header
- Import in session list
- Drag-and-drop import support

## File Location
`apps/web/src/lib/brenner-loop/export.ts`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] JSON export includes all data
- [ ] JSON import restores exactly
- [ ] Markdown export is readable
- [ ] Version mismatch handled gracefully

 {"id":"brenner_bot-1v26.5","title":"FEATURE: Personal Analytics Dashboard - Learning Patterns & Progress","description":"# Personal Analytics Dashboard - Learning Patterns & Progress

## The Gap This Fills
The system tracks behavior via GA4 for product analytics, but users have no visibility into their OWN learning patterns:
- How many hypotheses have I tested?
- What's my falsification rate?
- Which operators do I use most?
- Am I getting better at formulating testable hypotheses?

## Feature Design

### Personal Metrics
```typescript
interface PersonalAnalytics {
  userId: string;
  
  // Volume metrics
  sessionsCompleted: number;
  hypothesesTested: number;
  testsRecorded: number;
  
  // Quality metrics
  averageFalsifiabilityScore: number;
  averageSpecificityScore: number;
  hypothesesWithCompetitors: number;
  
  // Outcome metrics
  hypothesesFalsified: number;
  hypothesesRobust: number;
  hypothesesAbandoned: number;
  
  // Learning metrics
  objectionsAddressed: number;
  objectionsAccepted: number;
  revisionsAfterEvidence: number;
  
  // Process metrics
  operatorsUsedDistribution: Record<OperatorType, number>;
  averageSessionDuration: number;
  completionRate: number;
  
  // Trends
  trendsOver30Days: TrendData;
  trendsOver90Days: TrendData;
}
```

### Dashboard View
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  YOUR RESEARCH JOURNEY                                      │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                             │
│  ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════   │
│  OVERVIEW                                                   │
│  ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────   │
│  Sessions: 23        Hypotheses: 31       Tests: 87        │
│  Robust: 8 (26%)     Falsified: 12 (39%)  In Progress: 11  │
│                                                             │
│  ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════   │
│  LEARNING PROGRESS                                          │
│  ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────   │
│                                                             │
│  Falsifiability Score Trend:                               │
│  [Chart showing improvement over time]                     │
│  "Your hypotheses are becoming more testable! ↑12%"        │
│                                                             │
│  Objection Handling:                                        │
│  Addressed: 45    Accepted: 12    Dismissed: 8             │
│  "You take objections seriously — good sign."              │
│                                                             │
│  ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════   │
│  OPERATOR USAGE                                             │
│  ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────   │
│  Level Split:      ████████████░░░ 78%                     │
│  Exclusion Test:   ███████████████ 100%                    │
│  Object Transpose: ████████░░░░░░░ 52%                     │
│  Scale Check:      ████░░░░░░░░░░░ 26%                     │
│                                                             │
│  "Consider using Scale Check more — effect sizes matter!"  │
│                                                             │
│  ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════   │
│  GRAVEYARD INSIGHTS                                         │
│  ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────   │
│  Most common death cause: Reverse Causation (42%)          │
│  "Many of your falsified hypotheses failed due to          │
│   reverse causation. Consider this earlier."               │
│                                                             │
│  ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════   │
│  ACHIEVEMENTS                                               │
│  ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────   │
│  🪦 Hypothesis Hunter (5 falsified)                        │
│  🔬 Scientific Method (revised after evidence)             │
│  🎯 Precision Thinker (avg specificity > 80%)              │
│  🏆 Next: "Adversarial Mindset" (address 10 objections)    │
│                                                             │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Key Features

#### 1. Learning Curve Visualization
Show how the user's hypothesis quality improves:
- Falsifiability scores over time
- Specificity scores over time
- Time to formulate testable hypothesis

#### 2. Pattern Detection
Identify user-specific patterns:
- "You tend to skip Level Split — this may explain why 40% of failures are confound-related"
- "Your strongest hypotheses use all four operators"
- "You're faster at formulating hypotheses than average"

#### 3. Comparative Benchmarks
Optional: Compare to anonymous aggregate:
- "Your falsifiability score is in the top 20%"
- "Most users take 45 minutes for Full Analysis; you average 62"

#### 4. Personalized Recommendations
Based on patterns:
- "Try using Scale Check more often"
- "Your Object Transpose usage is low — reverse causation is your blind spot"
- "Consider the Adversarial Deep Dive template for your next important hypothesis"

### Privacy Considerations
- All analytics local by default
- Optional anonymized aggregate contribution
- Clear data export/deletion options
- No PII in stored analytics

## Why This is Critical
Without personal analytics:
- Users don't see their own growth
- Learning is invisible
- No feedback loop for improvement

With personal analytics:
- Progress is visible and motivating
- Patterns reveal blind spots
- Gamification drives engagement
- Meta-learning: learning about your learning

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Dashboard shows key metrics
- [ ] Trends visible over time
- [ ] Pattern detection provides insights
- [ ] Achievements are tracked
- [ ] Privacy controls work

 {"id":"brenner_bot-1vu","title":"Web API: sanitize recipients list in session kickoff","description":"## Problem
`POST /api/sessions` validates that `recipients` is a non-empty array, but does not validate that each recipient is a non-empty string after trimming.

## Why this matters
First principles:
- Input validation should be precise at the boundary.
- Avoids sending malformed payloads to Agent Mail and producing confusing downstream errors.

## Root cause
`apps/web/src/app/api/sessions/route.ts` maps `recipients.map((r) => r.trim())` without filtering empties.

## Fix
- Normalize recipients: trim, drop empty entries, optionally de-dupe.
- Reject if the normalized list is empty.

## Acceptance
- `recipients: [" "]` returns 400 validation error.
- Valid recipients still send successfully.

 {"id":"brenner_bot-28j","title":"Bug: Avoid duplicate window storage listeners during HMR","description":"Problem: apps/web/src/stores/readingStore.ts attaches a window "storage" listener with an inline handler and never removes it. In dev/HMR, this can lead to duplicate listeners and repeated updates; UBS also flags it.

Fix: Use a stable handler reference and remove any prior handler during module re-init (store handler on globalThis under a unique key), then add the listener once.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"PurplePond","created_at":"2025-12-30T08:41:43.595740468-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T08:49:59.096077737-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T08:49:59.096077737-05:00","close_reason":"Prevent duplicate readingStore subscriptions/storage listeners during HMR","labels":["reliability","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-28j","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.4","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-30T08:41:43.600166803-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
